title: Bresenham's Line Algorithm
description: Plot a line from (2, 3) to (10, 8) using Bresenham's line algorithm. Fill in the values for the step index k, the decision parameter P_k, and the plotted point coordinates (x, y).
hint: Start by calculating dx and dy. Here dx = 8, dy = 5. P_0 = 2*dy - dx = 2(5) - 8 = 2. Since P_0 >= 0, the next point is (x+1, y+1) = (3, 4). The next decision parameter is P_k + 2*dy - 2*dx.

title: Bresenham's Line Algorithm (Slope < 0.5)
description: Plot a line from (1, 1) to (9, 4) using Bresenham's line algorithm.

title: Midpoint Circle Algorithm (r=5)
description: Plot a circle of radius r = 5 centered at (0, 0) using the Midpoint Circle Algorithm. Fill in the trace table for the first octant (x from 0 until x > y).
hint: Initial point is (0, r) = (0, 5). P_0 = 1 - r = -4. Since P < 0, next point is (x+1, y) = (1, 5) and update P_1 = P_0 + 2*x + 3 = -4 + 2(0) + 3 = -1.

title: Midpoint Circle Algorithm (r=6)
description: Plot a circle of radius r = 6 centered at (0, 0) using the Midpoint Circle Algorithm.
hint: Initial point is (0, 6). P_0 = 1 - r = -5. If P < 0: plot (x+1, y) and update P = P + 2*x + 3. If P >= 0: plot (x+1, y-1) and update P = P + 2*x - 2*y + 5.
